page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd is a high-density compute instance powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors. It features 288 virtual CPUs mapped to 144 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The architecture includes a 504 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). The instance is equipped with 1080 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 3.75 GB per vCPU.

This configuration integrates a substantial local storage array consisting of 48 NVMe SSDs, totaling 19,031 GB of high-speed local capacity. In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, achieving high scores in stress-ng multi-core tests and Passmark CPU evaluations. Its memory subsystem delivers exceptional read bandwidth, exceeding 1,000 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Storage-bound and transactional performance is also notable, with Redis SET operations reaching 407.31 million operations per second and database operations placing in the top decile of tested systems.

While the platform excels in parallel processing, high-throughput web serving, and prompt processing for large language models, it exhibits a performance tradeoff in sequential text generation tasks, where LLM generation speeds fall into the average to weak categories. Given its massive compute density, high-speed local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix acceleration, the c4-standard-288-lssd is highly optimized for large-scale database deployments, high-performance computing (HPC) workloads, software compilation, and intensive data processing pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd is a high-density compute server featuring 288 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors, 1080 GB of RAM, and 19,031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.3 GHz with a 504 MB L3 cache, this instance supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory read bandwidth, and high database transaction throughput, with Redis SET operations reaching 407.31 million operations per second. However, a performance tradeoff is observed in sequential LLM text generation, which registers lower speeds. This server is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, high-performance computing, and parallel data processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd features 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1080 GB RAM, and 19,031 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and database throughput for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-288-lssd offers 288 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1080 GB RAM, and 19,031 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute and storage platform featuring 288 vCPUs and 19 TB of local NVMe SSD capacity for intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with 288 vCPUs and 1080 GB RAM.
Includes 19,031 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across 48 drives.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance.
Optimized for large-scale database deployments and high-throughput web serving.
